basics.name: customerSlug
basics.label: Customer Slug
basics.toolTip: A short lowercase identifier for your customer. Used to name Azure resources. Only letters, numbers, and hyphens allowed.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be 3-24 characters, lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only. Cannot start or end with a hyphen.

options.text: This step creates an App Registration in your Azure AD tenant with the 'Domains.Read.All' application permission. An admin consent URL will be generated at the end of deployment — an admin must visit it to activate the permission.

elements.name: appRegistrationName
elements.label: App Registration Display Name
elements.toolTip: The display name of the App Registration that will be created in your Azure AD.

options.text: Optionally provide an external API URL. After the admin completes consent, the Function App will POST the App Registration credentials (tenantId, clientId, clientSecret) to this URL. Leave blank to skip — credentials will still be visible in the deployment outputs and Function App settings.

elements.name: externalCallbackApiUrl
elements.label: External API URL
elements.toolTip: Your API endpoint that will receive a POST with { tenantId, clientId, clientSecret, customerSlug } after consent is granted.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid HTTP/HTTPS URL or left empty.

options.text: Review the deployment summary below. After deployment completes, check the 'Outputs' tab for the admin consent URL. Share that URL with a Global Administrator of the target tenant to activate the Domains.Read.All permission.

This deployment will create an App Registration in your Azure Active Directory. A Global Administrator must grant admin consent after deployment. The temporary Function App that handles the consent callback will self-delete after credentials are delivered.
